The breakthrough idea is well-motivated by nucleation-inhibition kinetics. Let me implement the Michaelis-Menten-style saturating suppression on the nucleation flux while keeping elongation intact.

Key structural insight from Abeta42 kinetics: inhibitors (like molecular chaperones) primarily suppress the primary nucleation and secondary nucleation *source* terms, delaying the lag phase, while fibril elongation (end growth) proceeds at similar rates once nuclei exist. A saturating (Hill-1) suppression captures dose-dependent lag extension that plateaus at high inhibitor.

- numerator is sum of squares → nucleation stays positive ✓
